prompt: Which AI startups have the fastest ARR growth on record in 2025-2026 (e.g., fastest to $1M, $10M, $100M ARR)? Name the companies, their ARR trajectory with timeframes, and what they sell. Cite sources.
content:
**Cursor (Anysphere) stands out as the fastest-growing by most accounts, with several other AI startups (especially in coding, voice, and video) achieving record trajectories to $100M+ ARR in 1–2 years or less during 2025–2026.**[[1]](https://www.tryanalyze.ai/blog/fastest-growing-ai-companies)[[2]](https://wellows.com/blog/ai-startups/)

These examples draw from 2025–early 2026 data in sources like Forbes AI 50, Sacra analyses, Bessemer reports, TechCrunch, and startup roundups. Traditional SaaS benchmarks (e.g., Slack or Zoom) took years longer for comparable milestones; AI-native tools have accelerated this dramatically due to product-led growth, virality among developers/creators, and low-friction adoption. Many “AI supernovas” averaged ~$40M ARR in year 1 of commercialization and ~$125M in year 2.[[3]](https://www.bvp.com/atlas/the-state-of-ai-2025)

### Cursor (Anysphere) – AI-Native Code Editor/IDE (Fork of VS Code)
- **Fastest B2B SaaS trajectory on record**: ~$4M ARR (April 2024) → $48M (Oct 2024) → **$100M ARR (Jan 2025, ~12–21 months from meaningful traction/launch)** → $500M (June 2025) → **$1B+ ARR (Nov 2025)** → **$2B ARR (Feb 2026)**. It reached $1B–$2B ARR faster than any prior software company (ahead of Slack/Zoom/Snowflake benchmarks).[[1]](https://www.tryanalyze.ai/blog/fastest-growing-ai-companies)[[4]](https://www.saastr.com/cursor-hit-1b-arr-in-17-months-the-fastest-b2b-to-scale-ever-and-its-not-even-close/)[[5]](https://thegtmnewsletter.substack.com/p/deconstructing-cursor-growth-playbook-4m-to-2b-arr)
- Sells an AI coding assistant/IDE that accelerates software development (autocomplete, agentic coding, codebase understanding). Used by enterprises like OpenAI, Spotify, Uber. Valued at ~$29B–$50B range in 2025–2026 rounds.[[2]](https://wellows.com/blog/ai-startups/)
- Founded ~2022–2023; small team driving extreme ARR/FTE efficiency.

### Lovable – AI “Vibe Coding” Platform for Full-Stack Apps
- **Extremely fast to $100M ARR**: Tied for first place at ~8 months from $1M to $100M ARR in some rankings. Reported ARR in the $200M–$400M range by late 2025/early 2026.[[6]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/matthewberman1_ai-saas-growth-activity-7391479396966187009-fsOI)[[7]](https://medium.com/jonathans-musings/ai-startups-you-should-know-48d69d1ddf61)
- Sells a natural-language platform where users describe apps in prompts (“vibe coding”) to generate full-stack applications. Positioned as a leader in AI-native app building.
- Swedish startup; high valuation (~$6.6B cited) reflecting rapid adoption.

### Replit – AI App-Building and Coding Platform
- Rapid acceleration post-AI agent launch: Grew annualized revenue from ~$2.8M to **$150M in under a year**; $10M → $100M ARR in ~5.5 months.[[7]](https://medium.com/jonathans-musings/ai-startups-you-should-know-48d69d1ddf61)[[6]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/matthewberman1_ai-saas-growth-activity-7391479396966187009-fsOI)
- Sells an AI-powered online IDE and agentic platform for developers and non-developers to build/deploy apps and software autonomously (Agent features highlighted as growth driver).
- Long-standing company (pre-AI pivot) that saw explosive growth in the AI era; raised at high valuation (~$9B cited).

### ElevenLabs – AI Voice Generation and Synthesis
- **~$100M ARR in ~2 years**; reached ~$200M ARR in 2025 (tripling valuation to $6.6B+).[[8]](https://linas.substack.com/p/top10aistartups2026)[[9]](https://www.indiehackers.com/post/tech/ai-startups-are-speedrunning-to-100m-arr-with-barely-any-people-on-board-qNIpbQUEz5cYymrkixsr)
- Sells AI voice cloning, text-to-speech, and generative audio tools for creators, enterprises, and media. Strong adoption in content/enterprise voice applications.
- Frequently cited alongside coding tools as a top hypergrowth example.

### Other Notable Mentions
- **Synthesia** (AI video generation): Surpassed $100M+ ARR by early–mid 2025, powering studio-quality AI videos for organizations.[[8]](https://linas.substack.com/p/top10aistartups2026)[[10]](https://www.leoniscap.com/research/the-leonis-ai-100)
- **Gamma** (AI presentation builder): Crossed $100M ARR with a small team (~50 employees).[[11]](https://www.forbes.com/lists/ai50/)
- Broader trend: Multiple AI startups (e.g., Anysphere/Cursor, Sierra, Glean) hit $100M+ ARR in under 2 years; Stripe data showed a surge in companies reaching $10M ARR in just 3 months in the 2025 cohort.[[2]](https://wellows.com/blog/ai-startups/)[[12]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/24/more-startups-are-hitting-10m-arr-in-3-months-than-ever-before/)

**Caveats**: ARR figures are often self-reported, estimated, or from funding announcements/roundups and can include usage-based revenue alongside subscriptions. Larger labs like OpenAI (~$25B+ annualized) and Anthropic (~$30B run-rate cited) have massive absolute scale but are typically not classified as early-stage “startups” in these growth-record discussions.[[11]](https://www.forbes.com/lists/ai50/) Data evolves quickly; these reflect snapshots through mid-2026 reporting.

Sources primarily include Forbes AI 50 (2026), Sacra/Cursor analyses, Bessemer “State of AI 2025,” TechCrunch round coverage, Substack/newsletter roundups, and LinkedIn/aggregate startup lists.
